titleStmt.title: SII 22.177: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 177. (A.R. No. 177 of 1906) SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, ELVĀNĀŚŪR. ON A ROCK NEAR THE (ANK CALLED) ŌṬṬAIKKUḶAM. VIKRAMA-CHŌḶADĒVA—9th YEAR.

summary.p: The inscription begins with the introduction Pūmādu puṇara etc. This records the remission of half of the perumpāḍikāval and śiṟupāḍikāval taxes payable to the chief Sūṟṟiyaṉ Irāmaṉ alias Rājendraśōḻa-Malaiyakularājaṉ, the Malaiyamāṉ of Kiḷiyūr from the dēvadāna lands belonging to the temples of Ūrbāgaṅkoṇḍadēvar, Badari-Āḻvār, Svarggāḻvār, Rājanārayaṇa-Viṇṇagar-Āḻvār, Vāṉavaṉmādēvi-Viṇṇagar-Āḻvār, Durggaiyār and Piḍāri at Iṟaiyānaraiyūr.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu vi
ḷaṅka jayamātu virumpat tan tirupāta malar mannavar
cūṭa manniya Urimaiyil maṇimuṭicūṭi ceṅkol ce
nṟu ticaitoṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali nikki meyyaṟan
taḻaippa kaliṅka miriyak kaṭalmalai kaṭātti valaṅkoḷāḻi varai
ūḻi niṟutti Irucuṭaraḷavum Orukuṭai niḻaṟkiḻc cem
pon virasimhāsanattu mukkokkiḻānaṭikaḷoṭum vi
ṟṟiruntaruḷiya kopparakecari panmarāna tribhuvanacakravartti
kaḷ śrī vikrama coḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu 9Āvatu malāṭāṉa
jaṉanātavaḷanāṭṭup pāṉūrkkūṟṟattu brahmadeya
m Iṟaiyānaraiyūrāṉa śrī coḻakeraḷaccaruppe
timaṅkalattu Ūrpākaṅkoṇṭaruḷiṉa tevaṟkkum
śrībadariyāḻvāṟkum tirusvarggāḻvārkkum vāṉavaṉ
mātevi viṇṇakarāḻvāṟkum rājanaārāyaṇa viṇṇakarāḻ
vāṟkum durggaiyāṟkum piṭāriyāṟkum Ivvūr pāṭikāvalu
ṭaiya kiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ cūṟṟiyaṉ Irāmanāṉa rājen
tracoḻamalaiyakularājaneṉ Ittevarkaḷ tevadāṉaṅkaḷ naṉ
cai puṉcai Ivvūriṟukkum perumpāṭikāval ciṟupāṭikā
val cempātiye śantrātittavaṟ Iṟukkakkaṭavitāka sam
matittu Uttarāyaṉa samkramattināḷ nirvārttuk kalveṭṭu
vitteṉ Ikkiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ cūṟṟiyaṉ Irāmanāṉa rāje
ntracoḻamalaiyakula rājaneṉ Ipparicāvatu Idharmmam Aḻippān
vallavaraiyaṉ satyame taṅkaḷammaikkut tāne miṇāḷan .
Digital edition of SII 22.177 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
